lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: Oh, hey, you have one of my crossbows! How's it working for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: This is Aleck's Hunter Emporium. Basically, it's just a shop with a fancy name; you can buy various weapons and traps here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I buy some equipment from the shop then?

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: Oh, this isn't my shop; the owner is Aleck over there behind the counter.

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: I experiment with weapon designs. I'm here because I've been trying to convince people to back my research and maybe sell some of my products - like this one I'm holding - in their shops.

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: Aleck doesn't seem to be interested, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about your crossbow?

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: It's good, isn't it? I designed it to incorporate the bones of various animals in its construction.

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: It's a fair bit faster than an ordinary crossbow too; it'll take you far less time to reload between shots.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's crazy, it'll never work!

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: That's what they said about my wind-powered mouse traps, too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And did they work?

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: Well, they only ran into problems because people kept insisting on trying to use them indoors.

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: Anyway, I think my crossbow invention is showing a lot more promise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds good. Let's trade.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the ammo for your crossbow.

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: Ah, I admit as a result of its... er... unique construction, it won't take just any old bolts.

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: If you can supply the materials and a token fee, I'd be happy to make some for you.

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: I need kebbit spikes, lots of 'em. Not all kebbits have spikes, mind you. You'll be wanting prickly kebbits or, even better, razor-backed kebbits to get material hard enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't I just make my own?

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: Yes, I suppose you could, although you'll need a steady hand with a knife and a chisel.

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: The bolts have an unusual diameter, but basically you'll just need to be able to carve kebbit spikes into straight shafts.

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: Meanwhile, since you're here, I can make some for you if you have the materials.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, can you make ammo for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be off now, excuse me.

lines.speaker: Leon
lines.text: Well, if you ever find yourself in need of that innovative edge, you can always find me here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... thanks.
